name: The Macallan 18 Year Sherry Oak
notes: treacle tart, stem ginger, raisins, sultanas, orange, cinnamon, dark chocolate, dried apricot, vanilla
best: Special occasion, Macallan loyalist, premium cigar celebration.
why: Recommend when Macallan 12 is not enough — sherry depth, fig, and chocolate for a milestone night.
catalogLine: The Macallan 18 Year Sherry Oak · Speyside, Scotland · $350 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: sherry, dried fig
blurb: Macallan 18 Sherry Oak — fig, ginger, and chocolate in deep sherried oak. The Speyside milestone pour beside a celebration cigar.

name: Maker's 46
mash: Wheated mash; seared French oak staves
notes: toasty french oak, caramel, baking spices, vanilla
best: Wheated fan ready for more oak, medium-full cigar night.
why: Recommend when wheated sweetness needs more oak and spice for a medium-full cigar.
catalogLine: Maker's 46 · Kentucky · $42 · 94 proof (47.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: vanilla, caramel
blurb: Maker's 46 — wheated bourbon with seared oak staves adding cocoa and spice. More structure than red wax without leaving wheated character.

name: Maker's Mark Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: vanilla, caramel, honey, orange peel, oak, baking spice, almond
research: Premium bourbon steady seller for gifting and sipping in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: Wheated bourbon fan, gift pour, red-wax recognition, medium cigar.
avoid: User wants high-rye spice bomb only.
why: Recommend when the member wants wheated sweetness with red-wax credibility — honey, vanilla, and soft spice.
catalogLine: Maker's Mark Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: vanilla, caramel
blurb: Maker's Mark — wheated bourbon with the red wax everyone recognizes. Honey and vanilla over orange peel when rye feels too sharp.

name: Mars Iwai Tradition
notes: gentle aroma, soft texture, solid body, sherry, plum, cherry, spice
best: Japanese whisky on a budget, sherry profile, medium cigar.
why: Recommend when Japanese sherry character is wanted without flagship pricing.
catalogLine: Mars Iwai Tradition · Japan · $45 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, sherry
blurb: Mars Iwai Tradition — sherry and plum in an approachable Japanese blend. Smart pour when the allocated shelves are empty.

name: Martell Blue Swift
notes: banana, caramelised pear, vanilla, coconut, spices, ginger, candied fruit, toasted oak
best: Member likes bourbon and cognac, crossover curious.
why: Recommend when the member loves bourbon and wants to try cognac — vanilla, caramel, familiar oak.
catalogLine: Martell Blue Swift · Cognac, France · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, grape
blurb: Martell Blue Swift — cognac finished in bourbon barrels. The bridge pour for bourbon drinkers who are cognac-curious.

name: Martell Cordon Bleu
notes: candied plum, apple, orchard fruit, spice, fruit, long finish
best: Cognac collector, floral-fruity luxury, maduro night.
why: Recommend when the member wants classic Martell luxury — fig, jasmine, and fruit cake depth.
catalogLine: Martell Cordon Bleu · Cognac, France · $220 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fig, jasmine
blurb: Martell Cordon Bleu — fig and jasmine in a cognac classic. Luxury for members who already know Hennessy and Rémy XO.

name: Michter's US*1 Kentucky Straight Bourbon
notes: caramel, vanilla, stone fruit, smoky depth, oak
best: Polished bourbon request, gift pour, medium-full cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants premium Kentucky bourbon — stone fruit, honey, and polished oak.
catalogLine: Michter's US*1 Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $48 · 91.4 proof (45.7% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: stone fruit, vanilla
blurb: Michter's US*1 Bourbon — stone fruit and honey over polished oak. Small-batch Kentucky that reads luxury without Van Winkle theater.

name: Michter's US*1 Straight Rye

why: Recommend when the member wants premium rye — citrus, spice, and polished oak.
catalogLine: Michter's US*1 Straight Rye · Kentucky · $48 · 84.8 proof (42.4% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: citrus, rye spice

name: Monkey Shoulder Blended Malt Scotch

why: Recommend when the member wants approachable malt — vanilla, orange, and honey without peat fear.

blurb: Monkey Shoulder — vanilla and orange across blended Speyside malts. Scotch for people who think they do not like Scotch.

name: Montelobos Espadín Mezcal

name: Mount Gay XO

blurb: Mount Gay XO — banana and vanilla from Barbados in an elegant rum. Refined where Diplomatico is decadent.

name: New Amsterdam Vodka
notes: citrus, clean grain, light sweetness, mineral, smooth, faint vanilla
research: Major value/flavored vodka segment performer in 2025 (Royal Batch).
best: House vodka, citrus cocktails, budget-conscious member.
why: Recommend when the lounge needs an approachable house vodka — citrus lift without Grey Goose pricing.
catalogLine: New Amsterdam Vodka · California · $15 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, clean grain
blurb: New Amsterdam — citrus-leaning grain vodka in the value tier America actually buys. Fine for highballs beside a mild smoke.

blurb: Nikka Coffey Grain — tropical sweetness from a column still. Japanese whisky that is easy to love when malts are impossible to find.

name: Nikka From the Barrel

best: Japanese whisky fan, cask strength, bold cigar.
why: Recommend when Japanese whisky should hit hard — citrus, spice, and 100+ proof balance.
